hub_subtitle: Business English para seguros, reuniões e entrevistas — e o desbloqueio da fala, do primeiro dia ao último

lesson.menu_title: Talking About the Future -- Plans and Intentions with 'Going To'
lesson.menu_desc: A aula em que o inglês, pela primeira vez em treze aulas, joga do lado dela. Ela vai falar do futuro — e o futuro em inglês é a única estrutura que é idêntica ao português. “Eu vou viajar” vira “I'm going to travel” — palavra por palavra: vou = going to. Nada novo pra temer. Nas doze aulas anteriores o inglês complicava (o does, o could, o how much); hoje ele simplifica. Ela leva seis palavras de plano (plan, future, next, soon, learn, change) e a estrutura completa do going to — afirmativo, negativo e pergunta. A virada: o David vem de Miami com um plano de verdade (o novo sistema, o treino em abril) e a Sofia fecha a aula com a frase que Paula precisa ouvir — “o futuro é seu” — e um plano real: a mesa internacional. -- 35 slides
lesson.subtitle: Lesson 13 -- Talking About the Future
lesson.title_tag: Professor View -- Paula Morales | Lesson 13 -- Talking About the Future
lesson.grammar_point: future with going to for plans and intentions -- affirmative (I'm going to + bare verb), negative (I'm not going to + bare verb) and yes/no questions (Are you going to...?)

listenings.text: Hi, Paula. David here. Before your call with Greg, I want to give you something, and it is a gift, because today the English is on your side for once. You are going to learn the future tense. I know that word scares you, but listen. In Portuguese you say it all day. Eu vou viajar. Eu vou aprender. Eu vou mudar. You already have it. In English it is the same thing, word for word. I am going to travel. I am going to learn. I am going to change. Vou is going to. That is the whole rule, and there is nothing new to fear. Now let me tell you my plans, because I have some. In April I am going to visit Sao Paulo. I am going to bring the new policy system, and I am going to train your whole team on it. We are going to work together for three months, you and me. And here is the part I want you to hear. I am not going to speak fast, and I am not going to use hard words. I am going to speak like a normal person, because that is what your English is ready for now. See you in April, Paula. Start making your plans.

Good morning, Paula. Sofia here. I want to talk to you about the future, your future, and not about grammar this time. I have watched you for a year now. In lesson one, you could not say your own job in English. Today you are going to take a call with the director in Miami, alone, and you are going to be fine. That is not small, Paula. That is a year of work. So here is my plan, and I am telling you first. Next month I am going to recommend you for the international desk. The large accounts, the calls in English, the clients abroad. I am not going to do it because your English is perfect, because it is not, and it does not need to be. I am going to do it because you ask when you do not understand, and because you never guess a number. Those are the things that matter here. Now I want one thing from you. Tonight, think about your own plans. Where are you going to be in one year? What are you going to learn? Do not tell me you are too old, or that it is too late, because I have watched you prove that wrong for twelve lessons. See you Monday, Paula. The future is yours.

items.situation: A colleague asks about your weekend. You have a plan: a trip to the coast. Tell her, using going to.
tips: I'm going to travel to the coast.
This weekend I'm going to visit the coast with my husband.

items.situation: Greg asks if you're going to be on the call on Monday. You are. Answer with a full sentence, not just 'yes'.
tips: Yes, I'm going to be on the call.
Yes -- I'm going to join the call at nine.

items.situation: Sofia asks about your plans for next year. You want to learn the new system. Tell her.
tips: Next year I'm going to learn the new system.
I'm going to learn the new system and take the international accounts.

items.situation: A broker asks if you're going to change jobs. You are not -- you like Zurich. Say it. This one needs the negative.
tips: No, I'm not going to change jobs.
I'm not going to change jobs -- I'm happy here.

items.situation: You want to ask Greg about his plans for April. Ask him what he is going to do.
tips: What are you going to do in April?
Greg, are you going to visit Sao Paulo in April?

items.situation: The client asks if the price is going to change soon. It is not. Answer, and use going to.
tips: No, the price is not going to change.
The price is not going to change this year.

bank.label: Talking about your plans / Falar dos seus planos
items: I'm going to travel next month.
I'm going to learn the new system.
Next year I'm going to take the international accounts.
I'm not going to change jobs.
Soon I'm going to speak English on every call.

bank.label: Asking about the future / Perguntar sobre o futuro
items: What are you going to do next?
Are you going to visit Sao Paulo?
When are you going to send the report?
Is the price going to change?
Where are you going to be in one year?
